commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=04ae9b212695d1aedd09880c4017b3d550524f81)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds top-level and per-account Feishu `cardFooter` config, wires it into streaming and structured-card note rendering, updates docs/tests/manifest metadata, and changes the shared progress draft compositor.

PR surface: Source +35, Tests +63, Docs +6. Total +104 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this PR adds a new Feishu footer customization capability. Source inspection confirms current main and v2026.6.9 do not have `cardFooter`; the PR body includes redacted live readback proof for the footer behavior on an earlier head.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the remaining blockers combine contributor current-head proof, a conflicting branch, and maintainer product approval for a new public config surface.

target.title: feat(feishu): configure card footer
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Feishu agent replies already render structured cards with a gray note footer, but operators cannot customize that footer from config. This makes the main agent card footer fixed to the generated `Agent / Model / Provider` metadata instead of allowing a deployment-specific label or operational note.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a Feishu plugin `cardFooter` config field and wires it into the existing structured-card note rendering path instead of adding a new card layout. The setting is shared config, so it works at `channels.feishu.cardFooter` and can be overridden per account with `channels.feishu.accounts.<id>.cardFooter`.

When unset or blank, Feishu cards keep the current generated footer. That preserves existing behavior while giving operators a small, explicit customization hook for main agent cards and other agent reply cards.

## User Impact

Users can configure a custom footer note for Feishu/Lark agent cards. The value is used on both streaming cards and complete structured-card replies. Existing configs continue to show the current generated `Agent: ... | Model: ... | Provider: ...` footer when `cardFooter` is not set.

## Evidence

Live Feishu/Lark behavior proof on PR head `1ebecc1156ea56026f507274dbf894d45bf93864`:

```json
{
  "pr": 95479,
  "head": "1ebecc1156ea56026f507274dbf894d45bf93864",
  "target": "user:o…e7cf",
  "accountId": "default",
  "staticCompleteCard": {
    "footer": "OpenClaw PR95479 footer proof 20260621043626",
    "sent": true,
    "messageId": "om_x10…39fc",
    "readbackContentType": "interactive",
    "readbackContainsFooter": true,
    "readbackExcerpt": "Static complete card footer proof for PR95479. stamp=20260621043626\n<font color='grey'>OpenClaw PR95479 footer proof 20260621043626</font>"
  },
  "streamingCard": {
    "footer": "OpenClaw PR95479 streaming footer proof 20260621044718",
    "cardId": "765370…0586",
    "messageId": "om_x10…5448",
    "closedVisible": true,
    "logs": [
      "Started streaming: cardId=<redacted>, messageId=<redacted>",
      "Closed streaming: cardId=<redacted>"
    ],
    "messageGet": {
      "contentType": "interactive",
      "containsFooter": true,
      "excerpt": "Streaming card footer content proof for PR95479. stamp=20260621044718\nfinalized\n<font color='grey'>OpenClaw PR95479 streaming footer proof 20260621044718</font>"
    }
  }
}
```

Local verification:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/accounts.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.ts extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/accounts.test.ts extensions/feishu/openclaw.plugin.json docs/channels/feishu.md`
- `git diff --check`

## Related Issues

- Partially overlaps https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/60748
- Partially overlaps https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/93667
- Partially overlaps https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/59360
